children.heading: Informed written consent
children.label: A Informed written consent
children.text: A State, local educational agency, or other entity receiving funds under this subchapter shall obtain prior written, informed consent from the parent of each child who is under 18 years of age to participate in any mental-health assessment or service that is funded under this subchapter and conducted in connection with an elementary school or secondary school under this subchapter.

children.heading: Contents
children.label: B Contents
children.text: Before obtaining the consent described in subparagraph (A), the entity shall provide the parent written notice describing in detail such mental health assessment or service, including the purpose for such assessment or service, the provider of such assessment or service, when such assessment or service will begin, and how long such assessment or service may last.

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: C Limitation
children.text: The informed written consent required under this paragraph shall not be a waiver of any rights or protections under .section 1232g of this title

children.label: A
children.text: an emergency, where it is necessary to protect the immediate health and safety of the child, other children, or entity personnel; or

children.label: i
children.text: a child whose parent has not responded to the notice described in paragraph (1)(B); or

children.label: ii
children.text: a child who has attained 14 years of age and is an unaccompanied youth, as defined in .section 11434a of title 42

children.heading: Prohibited use of funds
children.label: b Prohibited use of funds
children.text: No funds under this subchapter may be used for medical services or drug treatment or rehabilitation, except for integrated student supports, specialized instructional support services, or referral to treatment for impacted students, which may include students who are victims of, or witnesses to, crime or who illegally use drugs.
receiving an evaluation or other service described under this subchapter; or
attending a school receiving assistance under this subchapter.
